sections.summary: Guidelines apply to claims made for any food (regardless of a specific Codex standard) and establish the principle that foods must not be described or presented falsely, misleadingly or deceptively, with the marketer required to justify claims.

Defines a 'claim' as any representation stating, suggesting or implying that a food has particular characteristics relating to origin, nutritional properties, nature, production, processing, composition or any other quality.

Specifies prohibited claims, including assertions that a food provides all essential nutrients (except for regulated products or where permitted by authorities), implications that ordinary foods or a balanced diet cannot supply nutrients, unsubstantiated claims, disease-prevention/treatment claims (unless in accordance with applicable Codex standards/guidelines or national law), and claims that could create fear about similar foods.
Gives examples of potentially misleading claims such as meaningless or incomplete comparatives/superlatives and vague hygienic-practice claims (e.g., 'wholesome', 'healthful', 'sound').
This is a section heading ('GENERAL GUIDELINES ON CLAIMS') and contains no substantive guidance in the excerpt provided.
Document identifier 'CAC/GL 1-1979' indicating the Codex guideline code and year.

Permits certain conditional claims subject to specified requirements: added nutrients only when consistent with Codex principles and national legislation; reduced/omitted nutrients similarly; descriptive terms ('natural', 'organic', etc.) to follow national practice and not contradict prohibitions; religious/ritual claims (e.g., Halal, Kosher) only if conforming to appropriate authorities; claims that highlight absence/non-addition are allowed provided they are not misleading and meet conditions (not covered by specific Codex requirements, normally expected by consumers, not substituted without disclosure, and permitted), and absence-of-nutrient claims invoke mandatory nutrient declaration under Codex nutrition labelling guidelines.
